name: Inverse of companion matrix derivative basis
statement_text:
If

  Ōá(őĽ) = [ 1   őĽ + őĪ1     őĽ2 + őĪ1 őĽ + őĪ2            ¬∑¬∑¬∑   őĽn‚ąí1 + őĪ1 őĽn‚ąí2 + ¬∑ ¬∑ ¬∑ + őĪn‚ąí1 ],

then

       ő®‚ąí1 = [ Ōá(őĽ)     1 
                        1! Ōá (őĽ)   ¬∑¬∑¬∑          1
                                              (n‚ąí2)! Ōá
                                                       (n‚ąí2)
                                                             (őĽ)          1
                                                                        (n‚ąí1)! Ōá
                                                                                 (n‚ąí1)
                                                                                       (őĽ) ] .

variables.name: ő®
variables.definition: A matrix (likely the eigenvector/generalized eigenvector matrix) such that ő®^{-1}Xő® is a Jordan block with eigenvalue őĽ.

variables.name: X
variables.definition: A companion matrix (or similar matrix) associated with a polynomial, as implied by the preceding derivation.

variables.name: őĽ
variables.definition: An eigenvalue of the matrix X.

variables.name: őĪ1, őĪ2, ..., őĪn-1
variables.definition: Coefficients appearing in the polynomial defining the companion matrix X (or related to its characteristic polynomial).

variables.name: n
variables.definition: The dimension of the matrix X (and thus of ő®).

variables.name: Ōá(őĽ)
variables.definition: A row vector function of őĽ, defined as [1, őĽ+őĪ1, őĽ^2+őĪ1őĽ+őĪ2, ..., őĽ^{n-1}+őĪ1őĽ^{n-2}+...+őĪ_{n-1}].

preamble: The preceding text discusses the Jordan decomposition of a companion matrix X via a matrix ő® whose columns are the eigenvector and generalized eigenvectors of X corresponding to eigenvalue őĽ. The corollary gives an explicit formula for the inverse of ő® in terms of derivatives of a specific row vector Ōá(őĽ).
